name: Maryland
notes_normalized: Cannabis is legal in Maryland under state law for recreational adult use. Medical cannabis is not broadly legal in Maryland. Cannabis remains federally illegal in United States.
notes_raw: style="background:#196E92;" | {{Hs|1}} The program, known as the Natalie M. Possession of between 1.5-2.5 ounces is punishable by a civil fine of up to $250, and over 2.5 ounces is punishable by up to a $1000 fine and up to 6 months in jail. Additionally, a companion bill triggered upon passage of the referendum included a provision to automatically expunge all cases in which possession of cannabis was the only charge, as well as allow for individuals incarcerated for cannabis possession to petition for resentencing. In 2010, Maryland had the fifth-highest overall arrest rate for marijuana possession in the United States, with 409 arrests per 100,000 residents, significantly higher than the national rate, which was 256 per 100,000 people. In Maryland, Black people were 2.9 times more likely than Whites to be arrested for marijuana possession. Previously, possessing such was a misdemeanor with a $500 fine and/or 90 days in jail. Under the law, people over the age of 21 who are accused of having less than 10 grams had to pay a fine not exceeding $100 for first-time offenders, $250 for second-time offenders, and $500 for third or subsequent offenders. The measure makes both civil offenses punishable by a fine of up to $500. State (2019), the Maryland Court of Appeals determined that "the mere odor of marijuana coupled with possession of what is clearly less than ten grams of marijuana, absent other circumstances," is not sufficient probable cause for police to arrest and search a person within the state. From January 1, 2023, until June 30, 2023, decriminalization was temporarily expanded from possession of less than 1 ounce (28 grams) to less than 1.5 ounces (42 grams), as a civil infraction subject to a $100 fine as provided by House Bill 837, prior to full legalized recreational use of 1.5 ounces or less on July 1, 2023, due to the passage of the 2022 Maryland Question 4 referendum. Under the new law, it is legal to possess up to 1.5 ounces of cannabis for recreational use, as well as to grow and maintain no more than two cannabis plants out of public view; however, possession of between 1.5–2.5 ounces is punishable by a civil fine of up to $250, and more than 2.5 ounces is punishable by up to 6 months in jail, as well as a criminal fine not exceeding $1,000.

state_modifiers.federal_conflict: Cannabis remains federally illegal in United States.
